subject: History
chapter: The Rise of Nationalism in Europe

activities_projects.title: European Leaders Collage
activities_projects.objective: Identify key leaders of European nationalism
steps: Collect pictures of Mazzini, Garibaldi, Cavour, and Bismarck
Paste them on chart paper
Label each with their main contribution

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Italian Unification
steps: Research key dates from 1848 to 1861
Draw a horizontal line representing the years
Mark events like the Proclamation of Kingdom of Italy
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of dates and events (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: German Unification Map
steps: Locate Prussia and other German states
Shade regions unified under Bismarck by 1871
Create a legend for the colours used

activities_projects.title: Revolutionary Idea Chart
activities_projects.objective: Recall core values of the French Revolution
steps: Write the words Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity
Draw symbols representing these ideas (e.g., Broken Chains)
Explain how they helped start nationalism

activities_projects.title: Leader Role Match
activities_projects.objective: Associate leaders with their specific roles
steps: Write leader names on one set of cards
Write their titles (e.g., 'The Sword', 'The Brain') on another
Match the pairs correctly

activities_projects.title: Red Shirts Symbol Art
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of volunteers in Italy
steps: Draw a 'Red Shirt' uniform
List Garibaldi's 'Expedition of the Thousand'
Describe why the Red Shirts were important

activities_projects.title: Revolution Word Search
steps: Create a 10x10 grid with hidden words like 'Liberal', 'Nation', 'Republic'
Give the grid to a partner to solve
Define each word found

activities_projects.title: National Symbols ID
steps: Observe images of Marianne (France) and Germania (Germany)
List the symbols they carry (e.g., Olive branch, Sword)
Write what each symbol represents

activities_projects.title: Women in Nationalism Script
steps: Research women's participation in the Frankfurt Parliament
Write a short dialogue between two women activists of 1848
Highlight their demands for political rights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Awareness of gender roles in history

activities_projects.title: Bismarck's Policy Card
steps: Write 'Blood and Iron' in bold letters
List the three wars fought by Prussia over seven years
Mention who became the German Emperor in 1871
